channel.title: computer-science on mahmood
channel.description: Recent content in computer-science on mahmood

item.title: networking
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) this document is still being constructed, it should not be used for reference yet. 1 storage units basic units table: unit description bit 1 or 0 byte 8 bits kilobyte 1024 bytes megabyte 1024 kilobytes gigabyte 1024 megabytes terabyte 1024 gigabytes petabyte 1024 terabytes 2 unit prefix table prefix exponent yocto \(10^{-24}\) zepto \(10^{-21}\) atto \(10^{-18}\) femto \(10^{-15}\) pico \(10^{-12}\) nano \(10^{-9}\) micro \(10^{-6}\) milli \(10^{-3}\) kilo \(10^3\) mega \(10^6\) giga \(10^9\) tera \(10^{12}\) peta \(10^{15}\) exa \(10^{18}\) zetta \(10^{21}\) yotta \(10^{24}\) 3 bandwidth network bandwidth is a measurement indicating the maximum capacity of a wired or wireless communications link to transmit data over a network connection in a given amount of time.

item.title: data structures
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) for most data structures i havent implemented deletion yet.. also its good to note that most of the code isnt optimized to hell, but it doesnt really matter since its only for education purposes and you should probably be using the data structures provided by the programming language’s standard library 1 properties of floor for every 2 normal numbers \(m,n\) and every real number \(x\) \[ \ceil*{\frac{\ceil*{\frac{x}{m}}}{n}} = \ceil*{\frac{x}{m \cdot n}} \qquad \floor*{\frac{\floor*{\frac{x}{m}}}{n}} = \floor*{\frac{x}{m \cdot n}} \] \[ \ceil*{\frac{\ceil*{\frac{x}{2^i}}}{2}} = \ceil*{\frac{x}{2^{i+1}}} \qquad \floor*{\frac{\floor*{\frac{x}{2^i}}}{2}} = \floor*{\frac{x}{2^{i+1}}} \] the following is true for every normal number \(n\) and any number \(x\) \begin{gather} \floor*{x}+n = \floor*{x+n} \qquad \ceil*{x}+n=\ceil*{x+n}\\ n-\floor*{x} = \ceil*{n-x} \qquad n-\ceil*{x} = \floor*{n-x}\\ \floor*{x} \geq \ceil*{x}-1 \qquad \ceil*{x} \leq \floor*{x}+1\\ \floor*{x} > x-1 \qquad \ceil*{x} < x+1\\ \floor*{x} \leq x \qquad \ceil*{x} \geq x\\ \floor*{x} = n \iff n \leq x < n+1 \qquad \ceil*{x} = n \iff n-1 < x \leq n \end{gather}

item.title: operating systems
item.description:
\(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{an}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{ng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\adj}{adj}\) \(
ewcommand\dif[1]{\:	extrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{ceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter loor{\lfloor}{floor}\) 1 operating systems todos DONE 1.0.1 homework 1 State “DONE” from “TODO” [2022-08-07 Sun 18:57:00] State “TODO” from [2022-08-07 Sun 18:57:00] 2 write struct to file #include <stdio.h> #include <stdlib.h> #include <string.h> struct person { int id; char fname[20]; char lname[20]; }; int main() { FILE *outfile; // open file for writing outfile = fopen("person.dat", "w"); if (outfile == NULL) { fprintf(stderr, "
Error opened file
"); exit(1); } struct person input1 = {1, "rohan", "sharma"}; struct person input2 = {2, "mahendra", "dhoni"}; // write struct to file fwrite(&input1, sizeof(struct person), 1, outfile); fwrite(&input2, sizeof(struct person), 1, outfile); if(fwrite !
